Title: Wedding trip ends as 2 men nabbed at roadblock with suspected ketum
Article:
KOTA BARU: Two men's plan to attend a wedding in Kelantan ended when they were detained at a roadblock in Lalang Pepuyu near Tanah Merah yesterday.
The men were detained by the General Operations Force (GOF) 9th Battalion under Op Taring Wawasan Kelantan at about 10.30pm after a greenish liquid, suspected to be ketum juice, weighing about one litre and worth RM30, was found in the vehicle they were travelling in.
GOF Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said officers on duty at the Lalang Pepuyu roadblock spotted a Perodua Aruz carrying two men behaving suspiciously.
He said the vehicle was ordered to stop, and a search led to the discovery of a black bag in the rear passenger footwell containing a green bottle filled with the suspected ketum liquid.
"They were detained and the seized items, along with the vehicle valued at RM50,265, were brought to the Komtak GOF at Taman Bakti for documentation purposes.
"The case has been referred to the Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department of the Tanah Merah district police headquarters for further action under Section 30(3) of the Poisons Act 1952," he said in a statement.
He said the duo claimed they were heading to Kelantan to attend a wedding, and that the vehicle had been borrowed from the 29-year-old man's elder brother.
"Meanwhile, the 29-year-old said the ketum belonged to the other man, who is also his brother-in-law, and claimed he was unaware that the younger man was involved in ketum consumption.
"During questioning, the 20-year-old admitted the ketum was his and said he began consuming it earlier this year, obtaining supplies from a man near his home in Kuala Lumpur," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM: New digital system in the works for Johor-Singapore border easing
Article:
MUAR: A new digital system, to be launched jointly with Singapore in January next year, that is expected to ease congestion at the Johor-Singapore border.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, however, stopped short of revealing details of the initiative, saying doing so during the ongoing Johor election campaign could breach election regulations.
"I have the solution, but if I reveal it now, it could be against election rules," he said during the 'Jom Tanya PMX, Temu Anwar' and Bukit Naning volunteers launch held in Bakri here today.
He said traffic flow, border systems and congestion at the Johor-Singapore crossings had improved significantly over the past three years, adding that the new digital system would be launched together with Singapore Prime Minister Lawrence Wong in January.
"Once the system is ready, InsyaAllah, travel from Singapore to Johor will be much faster," he added.
Responding to a participant's question on whether the government would improve border counter operations to reduce waiting times for Malaysians commuting daily to Singapore for work, Anwar said the government remained committed to addressing congestion through technological solutions.
"We are serious about resolving this issue. The use of advanced technology will help shorten the time people spend crossing the border," he said.
On Thursday, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said nearly
20 million QR code transactions
were recorded at Johor's two main land checkpoints since the rollout of the MyNIISe system.
He said the digital initiative had helped ease congestion at the Malaysia-Singapore border by encouraging the use of faster clearance lanes.
"As of June 28, MyNIISe had recorded 19.48 million QR code transactions at Bangunan Sultan Iskandar (BSI) and Kompleks Sultan Abu Bakar (KSAB), alongside 2.4 million downloads and 1.27 million users," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police: College skipped background check on employee in teen molest, sodomy case
Article:
SHAH ALAM: A college that hired a child sex offender, who is now suspected of molesting and sodomising two teenage students, did not conduct a background check before employing him.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Sarudin Samah said initial investigations found that the automotive college had failed to check whether the suspect had a criminal record.
"As a result, the college was unaware that the individual had a record involving sexual offences," he said when contacted.
Sarudin said police would record statements from the college's management to assist in the investigation.
He added that the suspect had been employed at the college for about two years.
So far, police have recorded statements from one of the complainants and the suspect's father.
"The suspect is currently remanded until July 11," he said, adding that police were also awaiting medical reports.
Yesterday, police announced the arrest of the man, who is suspected of sexually assaulting and sodomising two students.
The arrest followed a manhunt after police received reports from two 15-year-old boys.
The first complainant alleged that he was molested in a hostel toilet, while the second claimed he was sodomised at the suspect's house in Section 18 here.
The first case is being investigated under Section 14 of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017, while the second is being investigated under Section 377C of the Penal Code for carnal intercourse against the order of nature.
Both offences carry a maximum jail term of 20 years and whipping upon conviction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar-Onn Hafiz exchange just 'check and balance', not a quarrel, says Tok Mat
Article:
SEREMBAN: Barisan Nasional (BN) deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan has dismissed suggestions that a public exchange between Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi amounted to a quarrel, describing it as a normal part of campaigning.
"That is just the ups and downs of campaigning, there is nothing to it, it is normal. I do not see it as a quarrel," said Mohamad, who is also Umno deputy president.
Mohamad, popularly known as Tok Mat, said Anwar had merely rebuked Johor Menteri Besar Onn Hafiz, who then responded in kind, and that this back-and-forth was healthy rather than confrontational.
"There is no quarrel. In a campaign, sometimes it is like that. The PM rebukes the MB, the MB rebukes back — and that kind of mutual checking is good.
"That is what you call check and balance," he told reporters after officiating the 2026 Rembau Umno Division Youth, Wanita and Puteri delegates meeting here today.
On Friday, Anwar said it was inappropriate for Onn Hafiz to use the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) as campaign material for the Johor election.
Anwar said that the strategic project was a federal government initiative still going through the intergovernmental process between Malaysia and Singapore and should not be turned into political capital.
Yesterday, however, Onn Hafiz claimed that Anwar's statement on the
JS-SEZ masterplan document
was inaccurate and could mislead the public and investors.
Mohamad, who is also Foreign Minister and Rembau Umno chief, also debunked the narrative circulating in some quarters that voting for BN in the Johor election would help free former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak.
He said the outcome of the Johor election had absolutely nothing to do with Najib's situation.
"Datuk Seri Najib's position is a federal matter. We are a constitutional country with laws, and we must respect the law. It does not arise that if we win in Johor, Datuk Najib can be freed. That has absolutely nothing to do with it.
"So I hope Johor voters are not swayed or taken in by slander that if they vote BN, Datuk Najib will be freed. That is a separate matter entirely," he said.
Mohamad also said BN's campaign machinery was currently on very good momentum, but cautioned that it needed to be stepped up further given the attacks that were expected to intensify as polling day drew closer.
"BN's momentum so far is very good, but I hope the party machinery and their work are further strengthened because there are many attacks and we need to counter unreasonable attacks .
"There is a chance some people will fall for them.
"So explain things clearly, especially regarding the claim that if BN wins, Datuk Seri Najib will be freed," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Armed forces chief, IGP lead Kedah honours list
Article:
ALOR STAR: Armed forces chief General Tan Sri Malek Razak Sulaiman and Inspector-General of Police (IGP) Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail, head the list of recipients of honours and medals in conjunction with the 84th Birthday Celebration of the Sultan of Kedah, Al Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah, at Istana Anak Bukit here today.
Sultan Sallehuddin consented to the conferment of the Darjah Dato' Seri Setia Pahlawan Yang Amat Dihormati (S.S.P.K), which carries the title Dato' Seri Pahlawan on both prominent figures.
[[nid:1481088]]
For the conferment of the Orang Besar Enam Belas, Datuk Syed Khairol Anuar Syed Abidin was bestowed the title Dato' Jaya Perkasa, while Datuk Tan Hong Eng received the title Dato' Jaya Dermawan.
As for the Orang Besar Tiga Puluh Dua, Datuk Mohamad Che Nai was conferred the title Dato' Johan Bakti, while Datuk Seri Boon Som Inong received the title Dato' Biram Jaya.
Meanwhile, Kedah legislative assembly speaker Zubir Ahmad was conferred the Darjah Dato' Setia Sultan Sallehuddin Kedah (D.S.S.S), which carries the title Dato'.
Four people received the Darjah Dato' Setia Diraja Kedah (D.S.D.K), which carries the title Dato'. They are Syarikat Air Darul Aman (SADA) chief executive officer (CEO) Radzuan Osman, Alor Star Mayor Datuk Abdul Gafar Yahya, Malaysian Immigration Department director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban, and Fisheries Development Authority of Malaysia director-general Dr Abdul Salim Tajudin.
Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah was conferred the Darjah Dato' Setia Pahlawan (D.D.S.P), which also carries the title Dato'.
The Darjah Setia Sultan Sallehuddin Kedah (S.S.S) was awarded to two recipients, namely Kedah state executive council member Major (Rtd) Mansor Zakaria and PAS Youth Chief Afnan Hamimi Taib Azamudden.
A total of nine people received the Darjah Setia Diraja Kedah (S.D.K), including Kedah State Islamic Religious Affairs Department director Syeikh Mohamad Subhi Abdullah, Langkawi Development Authority (LADA) deputy CEO (Management) Maznim Ismail, Kedah Public Works Department director Mohd Fisal Ismail, and Muda Agricultural Development Authority (Mada) general manager Zulkifli Romli.
Also receiving the S.D.K were Health Ministry Dental Health Programme deputy director Dr Jamaliah Omar, Sultanah Bahiyah Hospital head of Neurosurgery Department Dr Adam Mohd Zakaria, the hospital's senior consultant general surgeon Dr Manisekar K. Subramaniam, Real Estate and Housing Developers Association (Rehda) Kedah/Perlis chairman Yeoh Su Guan, and Bismi Empire Sdn Bhd president Jaffri Ahmad.
In addition, Sultan Sallehuddin consented to the conferment of the Ahli Mahkota Kedah (A.M.K) to 31 recipients, Ahli Setia Pahlawan (A.S.P) to five recipients, Bintang Perkhidmatan Cemerlang Kedah (B.C.K) to 31 recipients, and Bintang Kebaktian Masyarakat (B.K.M) to 43 recipients.
His Royal Highness also awarded the Ahli Cemerlang Semangat Jerai Kedah (A.S.K) to five recipients, Pingat Perkhidmatan Cemerlang Kedah (P.C.K) to 55 recipients, Pingat Jasa Kebaktian (P.J.K) to 40 recipients, and Pingat Perkhidmatan Lama (P.P.L) to 15 recipients.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MPOB Research station to boost Melaka's palm oil industry: Ab Rauf
Article:
JASIN: The Malaysian Palm Oil Board (MPOB) will build a strategic research station on a 40.47-hectare site in Seri Mendapat, Sungai Rambai, costing between RM20 million and RM25 million.
Melaka Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh said the project, slated under the 13th Malaysia Plan, is aimed at boosting the state's palm oil industry.
"The state government has allocated the land for MPOB's research station, which is one of three major initiatives set to transform Melaka's commodity sector," he told reporters at Kampung Seri Mendapat today.
He outlined that the site will house a model plantation, an R&D centre, modern labs, a training facility, quarters for TUNAS advisory officers, enforcement teams and other support infrastructure.
He spoke after launching the Jelajah Ketua Menteri Sayang Rakyat and Hello Komoditi 2026 events, held under the Sungai Rambai Wakil Rakyat Untuk Rakyat (WRUR) programme. Also present was Sungai Rambai assemblywoman Datuk Siti Faizah Abdul Azis.
Ab Rauf said the facility will position Sungai Rambai as a hub for knowledge, innovation, and palm oil industry development, creating more jobs, skills training and economic spillover for the local community.
He added that the initiative aligns with the state government's vision to elevate the commodity sector from a mere source of income into a modern, competitive, high-value driver of the rural economy.
Separately, he noted that a 5‑km private smallholder farm road at Ladang Lembah Kesang, Mukim Semujuk, will be built under an approved RM400,000 allocation.
"It may just be a road to some, but for smallholders, it speeds up produce delivery to market, cuts operating costs and eases their mobility. This facility is expected to directly benefit over 200 smallholders," he said.
The state government will continue to champion the needs of the Sungai Rambai community, nearly 45 per cent of which is made up of farmers and smallholders, he added.
MPOB has rolled out the Smallholder Oil Palm Replanting Financing Incentive Scheme 2.0, offering eligible smallholders up to RM14,000 per hectare to replace ageing, low-yield trees with superior seedlings.
Repayment only begins in year five, easing upfront financial strain.
On another development, Ab Rauf said the state has sought RM200,000 from the federal government to upgrade an ageing watergate at Jeti Sebatu.
Meanwhile, RM350,000 drainage works along a 300-metre Sungai Sebatu outlet are already underway to curb flood risks.
These projects respond directly to flood complaints from the local fishing community raised during the Jelajah Ketua Menteri Sayang Rakyat and WRUR programme, he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Catastrophic' Super Typhoon Bavi set to hit US Pacific islands
Article:
UNITED STATES: People in Guam and the Northern Marianas moved to emergency evacuation centers and made last-minute preparations on Sunday, hours before a "super typhoon" was projected to bulldoze through the US Pacific territories.
Super Typhoon Bavi was forecast to roar westwards over the area from early Monday with winds of 260 kilometers per hour  – equivalent to a category 5 hurricane – and gusts of up to 315kph.
The National Weather Service called the typhoon "very dangerous", warning of "tropical storm force" winds from Sunday afternoon or evening and "catastrophic" damage near the system's center later.
"Significant flooding from torrential rains, and coastal inundation are expected," the NWS said, with projected waves of up to 35 feet – the height of a 10-storey building – creating "extremely dangerous" conditions at sea.
Pinky Cubacub, 55, said as she boarded up the windows of her eatery in Guam that she had lined up early on Saturday morning to buy US$500 worth of plywood at a lumber store.
"I cannot afford to lose so many days. It hurts," she told AFP. "Because I just started, whatever we're making right now is just for rent, utilities, and my people, and supplies. I don't even pay myself yet."
Call centre employee Arabella Paulino, 48, said: "My girls were saying to me it's scary. But it will be okay."
"My house is concrete, so the worst that can happen is a window could blow in," she told AFP.
Derma Soaladaob said she was going to a hotel.
"I have a concrete house but with the noise and the wind, it's scary," said the 51-year-old, who drives a school bus for the US military.
Japanese tourist Miku Sakurai, 25, was supposed to fly back to Tokyo with her friends on Sunday but their flight was cancelled because of the weather.
"We will stay in the hotel when the storm comes. I am scared," the office worker told AFP as heavy rain began to fall.
- 'PRAYING' -
The Northern Marianas in the western Pacific has a population of around 40,000, and nearby Guam – a separate US territory, although both are part of the Marianas archipelago – about 170,000.
Super Typhoon Sinlaku, which hit the region in mid-April, knocked out power for tens of thousands of inhabitants, uprooted trees, overturned cars and ripped metal roofs off buildings.
A cargo ship, the MV Mariana, suffered engine failure and overturned in the storm. The body of one crew member was recovered and five others were missing, presumed dead.
By Sunday morning, Bavi was forecast to pass nearest to Rota, a small island roughly halfway between Guam and Saipan, the Northern Marianas' main island and home to around 1,500 people.
"By working together and taking the necessary precautions, we can help protect our families, neighbours and community. We pray for the safety of our people," Rota mayor Aubry Hocog said.
Teams from the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) teams were on the ground in Guam and its distribution center was stocked with 1.1 million liters of water, 1.2 million meals, 6,700 cots and 90 generators.
The NWS said the "window to evacuate or seek shelter is closing", and Guam opened five evacuation centres in schools with total capacity of 1,900, primarily for those in vulnerable homes.
- EL NINO -
The world's oceans experienced their hottest June on record and could set fresh highs in the months ahead, the European Union's Copernicus Marine Service said on Wednesday.
Warmer oceans help tropical storms to intensify and add more moisture, which can fall as heavy rain.
The World Meteorological Organisation warned on Friday that El Nino, which typically occurs every two to seven years and lasts nine to 12 months, has already begun in the tropical Pacific and is likely to be strong.
The natural climate phenomenon warms surface temperatures in the central and eastern equatorial Pacific Ocean, bringing worldwide changes in winds, pressure and rainfall patterns.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sharon Teo banks on the late Salahuddin's legacy in Permas bid
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The experience she garnered working alongside the late Datuk Seri Salahuddin Ayub is the main strength for Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate Sharon Teo Siew Hui in her bid to win the Permas seat in the 16th Johor election.
The Amanah candidate vowed to continue the struggle of the former Pulai member of parliament if given the mandate.
She said her experience serving in the Pulai constituency under Salahuddin's leadership had shaped her to understand the people's needs.
"I have served under the late Salahuddin's mentorship since 2018 and gained extensive experience serving the people in the Pulai. That experience is my greatest strength and the best testament to my ability.
"I will carry forward his spirit and the values he instilled in Permas," she said after attending a gathering with the Permas Indian community in Taman Megah Ria, Kota Puteri, today.
Also present was PKR vice-president Datuk Seri R. Ramanan.
Teo said PH's Johor Untuk Semua (Johor for All) manifesto would form the basis of a specific manifesto for the Permas seat, scheduled to be announced tomorrow.
Her campaign promises include infrastructure improvements in Permas.
Teo faces a four-cornered fight against incumbent Permas assemblyman Baharudin Mohamed Taib from Barisan Nasional (BN), T. Vela (Perikatan Nasional), and Dr Zamil Najwah (Bersama).
Commenting on the four-cornered contest, Teo expressed confidence that PH could recapture the seat, backed by the performance of the federal government under Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
"I believe the results under Anwar's leadership is proof. Voters will choose a government capable of advancing Johor, she added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 4 years jail, fine for labourer who kidnapped man over brother's debt
Article:
Pix by Nik Abdullah Nik Omar
PASIR MAS: A 32-year-old labourer, who was among four men involved in the kidnapping of a man over his sibling's debt, was sentenced by the Sessions Court to four years' jail and fined RM6,000.
Judge Zulkpli Abdullah imposed the sentence after Mohamad Syamsul Azroy Che Anuar pleaded guilty to charges of kidnapping the victim and stealing the victim's brother's car.
On the first charge, Syamsul, along with three others still at large, was accused of kidnapping Mohd Shahrul Nizam Mohd Asri, 35, from a house in Tendong on the evening of June 6.
The charge was framed under Section 365 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 34 of the same code, which carries a maximum prison sentence of seven years and a fine.
On the second charge, the accused and his three accomplices were charged with stealing a Volkswagen Jetta belonging to Shahrul's 25-year-old brother at the same time and location.
The charge was framed under Section 379(1) of the Penal Code, read together with Section 34 of the same code, which provides for up to seven years' jail and a fine.
According to the facts of the case, Shahrul was at home when a group of four men arrived in a Perodua Myvi and asked for his younger brother, Mohd Taufik Mohd Asri, over an unpaid debt.
When the victim informed them that his brother was not home, the group kidnapped him and fled with the Volkswagen Jetta.
The victim was confined at a house in Thailand before being released the following day and rescued by Thai police.
Deputy public prosecutor Siti Aiysha Na'ilah Harizan prosecuted, while the accused was unrepresented.
Earlier, Siti Aiysha Na'ilah pushed for a deterrent sentence, citing Syamsul's use of a weapon during the kidnapping and taking the victim across the border to Thailand.
The accused pleaded for a lenient sentence and requested it to run from the date of his arrest.s
Zulkpli sentenced the accused to four years' jail from the date of his arrest on June 16 and a RM3,000 fine, or three months' jail in default, for the kidnapping charge.
For the second charge, the accused was sentenced to three years' jail and a RM3,000 fine, or three months' jail in default.
The court ordered both sentences to run concurrently.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AmBank wins Fitch outlook upgrade
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Fitch Ratings has revised its outlook on AmBank (M) Bhd's long-term issuer default rating (IDR) to positive from stable, while affirming the rating at 'BBB-'.
The ratings agency also affirmed the bank's viability rating and government support rating at 'bbb-'.
"The positive outlook reflects our expectation that AmBank will continue to execute steadily on its business banking growth strategy, supporting its business profile and profitability, while maintaining asset quality performance," it said.
Fitch said the rating is anchored on AmBank's standalone profile, which reflects the consolidated credit profile of AMMB Holdings Bhd, as the bank is seen as having the same failure risk as the wider group.
It said AmBank's business profile has continued to strengthen, supported by consistent execution of its strategy.
Fitch said Malaysia's operating environment remains supportive, underpinned by resilient domestic demand and steady economic growth, although expansion is expected to moderate from recent highs.
AmBank has shown increasing appetite for SME lending, although risks associated with the segment have yet to materially affect asset quality indicators.
On asset quality, Fitch expects AmBank's impaired loan ratios and credit costs to remain stable in the near term, supported by a conducive operating environment.
"Asset quality risks are tilted to the downside due to lingering vulnerabilities in the SME sector, but we expect the impact to remain broadly manageable, given the bank's steady underwriting standards," it said.
Fitch said AmBank's risk-adjusted returns to remain broadly steady over the next 12-18 months, backed by steady loan growth and contained costs.
The firm said it may revise the outlook back to stable if AMMB fails to meet expectations and if further asset quality risks emerge from its business banking expansion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MOH plans permanent appointments upon housemanship completion from 2028
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Health Ministry aims to offer permanent appointments to medical graduates upon completion of housemanship from 2028 onwards, as part of efforts to resolve the long-standing contract doctor issue.
Health Ministry secretary-general Datuk Seri Hasnol Zam Zam Ahmad said the ministry is currently negotiating with the government to implement the policy, while progressively absorbing existing contract medical officers into permanent positions.
"We have projected the number of doctors still on contract. This year, 4,500 will be absorbed into permanent posts.
"In 2027 and part of 2028, we will absorb a few thousand more, around 2,000.
"We are now negotiating with the government to establish a new policy so that, beginning in 2028, all house officers who complete their housemanship will be offered permanent appointments immediately. That is the target," He said during the Bicara Sihat Edisi Khas podcast on Wednesday.
Hasnol said the ministry has sufficient vacancies to accommodate the appointments, although staffing movements such as resignations, transfers, further studies and spouse-placement requests continue to create operational gaps.
He said the ministry acknowledges that Malaysia still faces a shortage of doctors, as staffing requirements have increased alongside rising patient numbers and expanding healthcare services.
"Based on current staffing norms, some hospitals may require 300 doctors, but the approved establishment may only be slightly above 250. Even then, we are unable to fill every position immediately due to the time needed for housemanship and training.
"We are working to fill all existing approved posts while simultaneously applying for additional positions at facilities where the gap between staffing norms and approved posts is greatest."
Hasnol dismissed claims that recruitment into the ministry has been frozen, stressing that hiring is proceeding actively.
He said the ministry has been approved to fill 18,000 positions this year across its 86 service schemes, including doctors, nurses and allied health personnel.
"There is no recruitment freeze. We are working closely with the Public Services Commission to expedite the hiring process.
"As of June, nearly 60 per cent of the 18,000 approved positions had already been filled. We are confident all will be filled by year-end," he said.
He said the government is expected to approve several thousand more positions next year to support both existing healthcare facilities and new hospitals and clinics coming on stream.
On concerns over staffing shortages at hospitals, Hasnol said workforce management in the ministry is inherently dynamic due to promotions, retirements, resignations and officers pursuing specialist and subspecialist training.
He said these movements can temporarily leave hospitals understaffed until replacement officers become available.
"Sometimes several officers leave at the same time due to retirement, further studies or resignation. These are operational vacancies that occur dynamically.
"Hospital directors and state health directors may have to reshuffle staff temporarily while waiting for the vacancies to be filled," he said.
He said public concern over healthcare staffing reflects the importance Malaysians place on quality healthcare services, adding that the ministry remains committed to ensuring human resource planning keeps pace with growing demand.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BN parties may swap seats for Negri Sembilan polls, says Tok Mat
Article:
SEREMBAN: Barisan Nasional may reshuffle the traditional seat allocations of its component parties in the Negri Sembilan election on Aug 1.
BN deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said changing demographics required the coalition to adopt a bolder strategy to maximise its chances of victory.
"If possible, we want to give our BN allies the opportunity to contest seats they believe they can win," he said, adding that this would mark a departure from the long-standing practice of parties contesting seats traditionally allocated to them.
"So we want to review this to allow voters to choose the BN component party they prefer," he said after officiating the Rembau Umno Youth, Wanita and Puteri delegates' meeting.
Mohamad, who is also the Umno deputy president, said previous election results would serve as a guide in shaping BN's strategy for the state polls.
"If we received fewer votes (than in the previous election), why should the same party contest the same seat? There could be seat swaps," he said, adding that some component parties enjoyed stronger grassroots support in certain constituencies, which could improve BN's chances of winning.
Mohamad, commonly known as Tok Mat, said the proposal would ultimately be discussed by BN's top leadership before a final decision was made.
For now, he said all Umno division chiefs had been instructed to submit their proposed list of candidates after discussions at their respective divisional meetings.
"This is important to ensure transparency and avoid issues later on. Once a candidate has been named, everyone must accept the decision," he said.
Mohamad said infighting had cost Umno in previous elections, even when its opponents were not strong.
Separately, he did not rule out defending the Rantau seat, which he has held since 2004.
"However, I will leave the final decision to the leadership," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: Anwar admits weaknesses, vows gradual improvement
Article:
BATU PAHAT: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today admitted there are weaknesses in his administration but pledged gradual improvements for the benefit of the people.
Anwar said the government remains committed to combating corruption and abuse of power, and ensuring that errant leaders are held accountable.
"I agree there are things that are not right under my leadership, but we will fix them step by step.
"However, I want to make it clear that I will not tolerate theft. If you are a minister and you abuse your power, we will kick you out," he said during a meet-and-greet session with the rakyat in Senggarang near here today.
Also present were Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidates for the three Batu Pahat state seats, Felicia Poh Rui Ling (Penggaram), Onn Abu Bakar (Senggarang) and Mohamad Yazid Bakri (Rengit).
Anwar, who is also PH chairman, reminded the crowd that every citizen holds equal power through their vote, regardless of background or economic status.
"A person with a low salary, who has no car, rides a motorcycle and lives in a small house has the same power as a rich man or a big boss.
"That power is your vote. Use that vote to bring change to our country," he said.
He reiterated that his administration would not shield any minister or leader implicated in corruption.
He said past corruption among leaders had caused massive losses to the country, while the people continued to face a high cost of living.
Anwar urged voters to give PH a mandate in the Johor election to allow the reform agenda and administrative improvements to continue.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Visually impaired swimmer Faiq returns to Oceanman
Article:
TUARAN: Visually impaired swimmer Faiq Adnan returned to the Oceanman World Series here for the second consecutive year.
Competing in the 5km Inspiration Category, Faiq was accompanied by his swimming guide and coach, Datuk Ooi Win Juat, who swam in tandem with him and led the way throughout the race course.
"I want to show others that visual impairment or any physical disability is not an excuse for not achieving life's goals.
"One can still contribute actively to society and be an inspiration, no matter what card life deals you," he said in a statement.
The 36-year-old, who was born blind, has been swimming since the age of nine.
In 2013, he made history by becoming the first blind Malaysian to successfully swim across the Penang Strait.
Three years later, he competed in the 6km open category at the Bangkok Swimathon in Thailand, finishing 32nd out of 52 swimmers.
For Ooi, age or disability should never be an obstacle to pursuing a passion for open-water swimming.
Last year, he also accompanied Faiq to the Oceanman World Final Championship at Kite Beach, Dubai.
Faiq was among 458 athletes from 31 countries who competed in this year's Oceanman Kota Kinabalu, held at Dalit Bay over the weekend.
The championship attracted swimmers from countries including Russia, France, Mexico, Australia, India and the United States, while 180 of the participants were Malaysians, mainly teenagers.
The two-day event featured the Oceanman (10km), Half Oceanman (5km), Sprint (2km), Oceankids (500m) and Oceanteams Relay (3x500m).
The top 10 swimmers in each age group, as well as the top three finishers in the Inspiration Category for swimmers with a disability level of more than 33 per cent, will earn qualification slots to the Oceanman World Final Championship in Miches, Dominican Republic, from Nov 9 to 15.
Oceanman Malaysia race director Azura Zainol Abidin said organising the world-class event in Sabah for three consecutive years had been a labour of love.
"I am truly inspired and thrilled to see how an event like this has managed to create a large following among Malaysians, building a multi-generational community of swimmers who are passionate about open-water swimming.
"Most importantly, I am touched by the friendships that have been built through this event," she said.
As the curtain closed on Oceanman's three-year run in Sabah, the weekend celebrated sportsmanship, community spirit and the natural beauty of the state.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Stand with Onn Hafiz to continue developing Johor, Zahid tells youths
Article:
ISKANDAR PUTERI: Barisan Nasional (BN) hopes Johor's youths will continue to support caretaker menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, the coalition's poster boy for the July 11 state election.
BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said voters aged 18 and those below 40, including first-time voters, make up about 52 per cent of the electorate.
"I hope they will stand with Onn Hafiz to continue developing Johor. We are confident they are very close to Onn Hafiz, and that is why we have once again put him at the forefront.
"Insya-Allah, with the consent of the palace, he will continue as Johor menteri besar," he said.
The deputy prime minister was speaking to reporters after launching the Johor Darul Ta'zim Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) TVET Tour Programme, which was also attended by Mara chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki.
Earlier, in his speech, Zahid urged young Johoreans to build their careers in the state instead of seeking opportunities elsewhere.
He said Johor was on track to become more developed than other states.
"Do you want to stand with Onn Hafiz to build Johor's future? I believe the young people before me have the determination to do so.
"I believe that is also the (caretaker) menteri besar's greatest hope, to ensure the state's development not only attracts investment but also creates quality jobs for Bangsa Johor," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Langkawi not Phuket or Bali, says Sultan of Kedah
Article:
ALOR STAR: The Sultan of Kedah, Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah, will not compromise with any party that takes advantage of the situation by making statements or engaging in actions that could tarnish the image and reputation of Langkawi.
His Royal Highness said Langkawi is a strategic asset of Kedah and the nation, which must be preserved and developed sustainably to continue driving the state's economic growth.
"I want the development of Langkawi to be shaped according to its own identity, without imitating or being compared to Phuket or Bali.
"I will not compromise with any party that takes advantage by making any actions or statements that could tarnish the image and reputation of Langkawi.
"The true strength of Langkawi lies in its natural treasures and unique heritage. Therefore, this valuable asset must be preserved and developed sustainably," His Highness said at the 2026 Kedah State Awards and Honours Investiture Ceremony at Istana Anak Bukit today.
Sultan Sallehuddin said the state government and all relevant parties must ensure that all development efforts in Langkawi are carried out in a planned manner without compromising the island's identity and uniqueness as a resort destination.
The ruler also called on the federal government to review the lease payment rate for Penang based on current rates, in line with the spirit and original intent of the agreement.
"The lease payment for Penang received by my government is based on a long-standing agreement.
"However, the current rate is far lower than what it should be. I wish to remind all parties that the rights and sovereignty of the state of Kedah, as enshrined in the Federal Constitution of Malaysia, must be respected by all," he said.
Sultan Sallehuddin also called on the federal government to cooperate and ensure that there are no administrative obstacles in Kedah, including in the management of rare earth elements (REE).
His Highness said the state's rights and authority to manage and explore natural resources must be respected and facilitated by all parties.
"It would be a great loss if this matter fails to be implemented simply due to differences in political views and understanding, thereby disregarding the rights and needs of the state."
Meanwhile, Sultan Sallehuddin expressed concern over delays in the implementation of several strategic infrastructure projects, including the Sungai Kedah/Anak Bukit Flood Mitigation Project, which has yet to be completed after a long period.
His Highness said the delay has led to repeated flooding incidents affecting residents in the area.
As such, His Highness called for immediate action to be taken so that the people are no longer burdened by the same problems.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump celebrates America's 250th birthday, blasts 'communists'
Article:
WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump hailed America on its 250th birthday Saturday as the "crowning achievement" of human history, even as he used the event to renew his attack on domestic opponents he branded communists.
In a speech delayed by several hours when storms forced the temporary evacuation of crowds in Washington, Trump claimed that under his presidency the United States was "prouder than ever before."
While Trump had promised a huge political rally to stamp his brand on the national celebrations, the 80-year-old Republican largely stuck to a more traditionally patriotic script.
"For two and a half centuries, our American republic has stood as the crowning achievement of human history," Trump told tens of thousands of people on the National Mall.
On stage, he hailed also veterans from World War II and the Korean and Vietnam wars.
But he then hailed the latter two as examples of the battle against "communists" – reprising his message from his speech on Friday night at the iconic Mount Rushmore monument.
"Our warriors did not fight communism on battlefields across the word, only to have that menace rear its ugly head right back here in America. We're not going to let it happen," he said.
- 'PROUD TO BE AMERICAN' -
Trump has repeatedly hammered home the theme recently ahead of November's US midterm elections, after the anti-establishment left of the Democratic Party won a string of US primary victories.
"It's like a cancer, you've got to cut it out," he added.
The US leader also used the speech to boast of the recent military campaigns against Iran and Venezuela, saying Washington had "wiped out" Teheran's military.
But the speech was short by Trump's standards, at around 45 minutes long.
"We love Trump, we loved his speech," said Richard Sullivan, 70, who came from Virginia with his wife Nancy and was wearing a Freedom 250 T-shirt.
"He went through our great history and had a word for the veterans that were here. He makes us proud to be American."
A huge fireworks display began after the speech. Trump has said it will set a record as the world's largest.
Americans braved a scorching heatwave in cities across the east of the country to celebrate the anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence from Britain in 1776.
Temperatures soared to a record 39.4C in the capital – an all-time high for July 4 – with 160 million Americans under extreme weather warnings, according to the National Weather Service.
Inclement weather meanwhile forced fireworks in New York to be pushed earlier, and resulted in evacuations at a concert in Philadelphia and a celebration on the Charles River in Boston.
- 'AWESOME COUNTRY' -
The elements also threatened to wreak havoc with Trump's speech as tens of thousands were ordered to clear the National Mall several hours before his address due to approaching thunderstorms.
While many streamed toward exits, chaos broke out as other attendees refused to leave or tried to surge back in, where AFP reporters witnessed shouts of "charge!" and "Trump! Trump!"
Trump however insisted he was pushing ahead with the speech, telling Fox News that if D-Day veterans in World War II could brave bad weather then so could he.
"Experiencing a little heat is a lot less than what a lot of people sacrifice to give us this freedom in this awesome country," said Randy Cole, 62, a retired civil servant attending festivities in Washington.
Yet Trump's determination to put himself and his political brand front and centre of the 250th anniversary celebration had highlighted the deep divisions caused by his second term in office.
Those divisions were on full display Saturday near Washington's Capitol Hill, where masked men – some of them carrying Confederate flags and others sporting logos of the white supremacist Patriot Front – gathered to shout, "Reclaim America!"
For Americans, the 250th anniversary offers a moment for reflection as well as celebration.
A Quinnipiac University Poll showed 61 per cent of Americans thought the US was not living up to the ideals stated in the Declaration of Independence.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Saudi non-oil sector growth accelerates in June as new orders hit four-month high, PMI shows
Article:
RIYADH: Saudi Arabia's non-oil private sector strengthened its expansion in June, buoyed by the sharpest pick-up in new business in four months, though firms continued to grapple with steep cost pressures and weakening foreign demand, a survey showed on Sunday.
The seasonally adjusted Riyad Bank Saudi Arabia Purchasing Managers' Index rose to 53.3 in June from 52.8 in May, marking the highest reading in four months and signalling a solid improvement in operating conditions at the close of the second quarter.
Output growth held broadly steady, with roughly 18 per cent of surveyed firms reporting higher activity against just two per cent recording declines.
Panellists credited fresh project approvals, stronger customer demand and the revival of orders previously delayed by conflict in the region.
New business volumes climbed at the fastest pace since February, as easing geopolitical concerns lifted investor confidence and domestic spending.
"Stronger output and the fastest increase in new orders in four months suggest that business activity regained momentum towards the end of the second quarter," said Naif Al-Ghaith, chief economist at Riyad Bank.
Not all indicators were rosy, however. Export orders contracted steeply for a fourth consecutive month amid logistical bottlenecks and stiff foreign competition, while hiring stagnated.
Meanwhile, input costs capped their steepest quarter in 15 years, prompting firms to raise output charges at the second-squickest rate in nearly six years.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Uber pauses Europe food delivery expansion as it pursues Delivery Hero deal
Article:
NEW YORK: Uber has paused the majority of its planned food delivery expansion in Europe just months after announcing the plans as the ride-sharing company continues to pursue a takeover of Delivery Hero, the Financial Times reported on Sunday.
Uber no longer plans to launch food delivery in five of the seven countries it had targeted for expansion this year, including Austria, Norway and Greece, the report said. It did not identify the other two countries.
Earlier in the year, Uber said it planned to expand its food delivery business into seven new European markets - Austria, Denmark, Finland, Norway, the Czech Republic, Greece and Romania.
The company expected the move to generate an additional US$1 billion in gross bookings over the next three years.
The San Francisco-based company, however, is still seeking to acquire Delivery Hero, according to the FT report.
Delivery Hero said in May it had received a €33-per-share (US$37.74) offer from Uber, and Reuters reported that the US company had raised its stake in the German food delivery company to nearly 37 per cent from 25 per cent after buying a stake from fellow shareholder Aspex Management.
Reuters could not verify the FT report. Uber and Delivery Hero did not immediately respond to requests for comment.
Uber told the FT it had decided to halt its expansion after the "huge success" of launches in Finland and Denmark, with plans to "focus on continuing the momentum" in existing markets.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: OPEC+ tipped to raise quotas again as Middle East calms
Article:
LONDON: Seven OPEC+ members are likely to raise oil production quotas on Sunday as Gulf countries are reeling from the Middle East war, analysts told AFP.
Saudi Arabia, Russia and five other members of the enlarged Organisation of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC+) are meeting online on Sunday to discuss quotas for August.
OPEC+ will likely continue "to unwind the production cuts at the same pace like in the previous months", said Giovanni Staunovo, a commodity analyst at the Swiss bank UBS, predicting an increase of 188,000 barrels per day.
"But for now, production is probably still below the group's targets," Staunovo told AFP.
Gulf countries had to cut output after the near-paralysis of the Strait of Hormuz orchestrated by Iran during the war in the Middle East, which blocked their oil exports for several months.
Between the first quarter of 2026 and May, combined production by Saudi Arabia, Iraq, and Kuwait – three of the seven countries raising their quotas – fell by some six million barrels per day, OPEC data have shown.
But on June 17, Tehran and Washington signed a memorandum of understanding, committing themselves to removing obstacles to maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz for the duration of talks following the signature.
Time-consuming restart
Since then, ship transport in the region has shown signs of recovery, and oil prices have dropped sharply to levels comparable to those seen before the war in anticipation of a gradual return to normal.
Oil supplies through this shipping lane may already have exceeded ten million barrels a day, according to a US official quoted by the Bloomberg agency.
But the oil currently leaving the strait has up to now been stored on tankers or in storage facilities, said Saxo Bank analyst Ole Hansen, adding that "shut-in production takes time to restart."
"Assuming shipping continues to normalise, July will show an improvement with August probably being the month where the pickup accelerates," he told AFP.
Cohesion at stake
"For next year, everybody is anticipating a surplus," Jorge Leon, an analyst at Rystad Energy, told AFP.
Rebuilding the inventories that countries tapped during the conflict should help absorb the flows at first, but producers may face a strong downward pressure on prices later on.
And OPEC+, already weakened by the departure of the United Arab Emirates from the group in May, will have to manage sliding prices while members will push for production increases.
Iraq, in particular, has asked the cartel to raise production quotas to make up for the shortfall it incurred during the war in the Middle East, the Iraqi Oil Ministry said in late June.
But Hansen said the need for a higher quota "is not imminent" as production volumes are still far from their pre-conflict levels.
"Iraq's request may become part of the 2027 capacity review, where production baselines will be examined," he added.
At the end of the year, the OPEC+ is indeed due to reassess members' quotas based on their ability to produce more, which could become a thorny issue.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: TVET Commission establishment to be finalised by end 2026
Article:
ISKANDAR PUTERI: The establishment of the National Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) Commission is expected to be finalised by the end of this year.
Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said engagement sessions on the commission's establishment were ongoing.
"I think by the end of this year, we will finalise everything and table it in the Dewan Rakyat and Dewan Negara.
"Once the engagement sessions are completed, we will submit a paper to the cabinet for approval," he said after launching the Johor Darul Ta'zim Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) TVET Tour Programme.
Zahid, who is also the rural and regional development minister, said policy approval for the commission had already been obtained.
"Although policy approval has been secured, it still needs to be brought to Parliament because it involves legal matters."
The National TVET Commission is intended to coordinate and strengthen the country's skills ecosystem.
It is expected to serve as a centralised body to monitor and coordinate efforts to produce highly skilled workers who meet industry needs.
On today's programme, which was also attended by Mara chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki, Zahid said it was organised to promote TVET education programmes.
"We see that the employability rate of Mara TVET graduates is about 99.5 per cent, which is our highest achievement so far, and we aim to reach 100 per cent," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Syariah Court postpones trial of 14 men charged with dressing as women
Article:
KOTA BARU: The Syariah Lower Court today postponed the trial of 14 men charged with dressing as women at an apartment in Jalan Mahmood, here, last year.
Syarie Judge Wan Mohd Firdaus Wan Ibrahim fixed Sept 20 for the continuation of the trial.
The 14 men are represented by lawyers Muhammed Syarafi Mohd Salleh, Mohd Faqqhuruddin Mohd Nasir and Muhammad Amirul Ashraf A. Ghani, while the prosecution is led by Kelantan Syarie prosecuting officer Syafiqah Mat Husin.
Muhammed Syarafi told reporters the postponement was due to technical arrangements to allow all 14 accused to be tried together before a different judge in a larger courtroom.
He said the 14 are facing the same charge under Section 19(1) of the Kelantan Syariah Criminal Code Enactment 2019.
"Today's proceedings involved technical issues and court requested that the hearing be moved to a larger courtroom.
"The court also directed that all accused facing the same charge be tried together.
"We also requested several documents from the prosecution, including the list of witnesses," he said.
Muhammed Syarafi said the prosecution is expected to call four witnesses when the trial resumes.
The 14 were alleged to have dressed like women by wearing tight long-sleeved clothing, tight pants, high-heeled shoes and short skirts above the thigh at the multipurpose hall on the sixth floor of Troika Residence in Kota Baru.
They claimed trial in April last year and were released on bail of RM1,000 each with one surety.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar urges Johor voters to give PH a chance
Article:
BATU PAHAT: Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged Johor voters to give the coalition a chance to govern the state, citing unresolved public issues under the Barisan Nasional (BN) administration.
Anwar said the BN-led state government had neglected the people's interests, particularly over rising housing costs.
"We manage the federal government, and we look after their (Johor) government. There are many development projects in Johor, but public issues remain unresolved.
"I see government officers, police and military personnel in Johor without homes, even though billions of ringgit in development are being recorded.
"Affordable housing is the responsibility of the state government, but it has failed to deliver. Therefore, I must intervene and ask for a state government aligned with PH so we can develop public housing," he said during a meet-the-people session for the Senggarang state constituency here today.
Also present were three PH state election candidates – Felicia Poh (Penggaram), Onn Abu Bakar (Senggarang) and Mohamad Yazid Bakri (Rengit).
Anwar also called on voters to embrace change and give PH the opportunity to govern the state.
"We need good governance to transform Johor for the benefit of the people. You receive STR (Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah) and Sara (Sumbangan Asas Rahmah), but do you have to queue up?" he asked.
He said he could not bring about change alone without the support of voters.
"I cannot do it alone. I can work hard, help the people and stop corruption, but now even those who steal are elevated as leaders.
"Have I taken land? Taken projects? No. We want to protect Johor because this is not a poor state – it is wealthy, yet many of its people remain poor.
"We will resolve the people's problems. We have settled the highway issues, but we must also assist the public with housing, food and aid.
"That is why the state government must align with the federal administration. PH must be given the chance to govern so we can bring about change.
"I came to campaign because I believe this state can do better. In Johor, sometimes it is not just places of worship – even mosques and surau face problems. Roads are under state jurisdiction, whereas we have already spent tens of billions on federal responsibilities," he said.
Anwar added that the people must elect capable and courageous leaders to ensure Malaysia's safety and foster unity among all races.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] PM: Govt to boost support for AIM to reach more needy groups
Article:
BATU PAHAT: The government is increasing support for Amanah Ikhtiar Malaysia (AIM) to ensure no group, especially those in rural areas, is left out, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
"People often talk about helping the rakyat. Everywhere they go, especially during election campaigns, they say they want to help. But in the end, not all of it reaches the people, or only a small portion does.
"Amanah Ikhtiar has been funded from the beginning. I remember the first allocation was RM200 million, without repayment.
"We spend tens of billions on infrastructure, highways and the like, but for the rural people, this is the real effort," he said during the Kenduri Rakyat Bersama PMX programme at Vila Kemboja, Semerah, today.
He also praised AIM's management for elevating the organisation into one of the world's best poverty eradication initiatives.
Also present were Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari, AIM Board of Trustees chairman Datuk Seri Dr Syed Hussain Syed Aljunid, and Pakatan Harapan (PH) Semerah candidate Mohd Khuzzan Abu Bakar.
Anwar, who is also finance minister, said public support for the unity government would be channelled back to the people through programmes under AIM.
He also said no other prime minister had attended AIM programmes as frequently as he had to support rural communities.
"Others may dismiss it, saying AIM is just for rural people and not worth focusing on.
"Since I became prime minister, I have attended eight AIM programmes. No other prime minister has done that.
"Why? Because while we talk about the future of the country, we must remember promises made over decades. If we do not make the right decisions, we will be left behind," he said.
Anwar said that each time he attended AIM programmes, the government would increase its allocation to the organisation.
"Each time we meet, we add RM20 million. In the Budget, we add RM50 million. AIM keeps asking me for funds, but it is fine because it is not for me or for them, but for the people," he said, adding that the government was working to improve military quarters, some of which had not been upgraded for decades.
"There are military quarters last repaired 30 or 40 years ago. Some have never been renovated since the colonial era.
"So I have asked the Royal Army Engineers Regiment (to) spend hundreds of millions each year (to contact repair works)," he said.
He said the state and federal governments should be aligned to ensure smoother delivery of development and assistance to the people.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Synthetic drugs fuelling mental health crisis among young Malaysians
Article:
LETTER:
I am concerned by the National Anti-Drug Agency (Nada) finding that synthetic drugs now make up 73 per cent of drug abuse cases in Malaysia, with more young users experiencing mental health problems, particularly through vape misuse.
This alarming trend should serve as a wake-up call to all Malaysians. Synthetic drugs are no longer merely a drug abuse problem.
They have evolved into a serious public health, mental health and social crisis.
Unlike conventional drugs, synthetic drugs are manufactured using dangerous chemical compounds that are often far more potent and unpredictable. Their effects on the brain can be devastating.
Frequent use can damage brain function and alter normal chemical activity, increasing the risk of severe mental health conditions such as anxiety disorders, depression, paranoia, hallucinations, psychosis, violent behaviour and, in some cases, long-term schizophrenia-like symptoms.
This is what medical experts commonly refer to as "dual diagnosis", where an individual suffers simultaneously from substance use disorder and a mental illness.
Such patients are much more difficult to treat because both conditions interact and reinforce each other. If addiction is not addressed, mental illness becomes harder to manage.
Conversely, untreated mental illness often leads to continued drug dependence.
The growing use of synthetic drugs through vape devices is particularly disturbing.
Many young people are unaware that what they are inhaling may contain dangerous synthetic substances that can rapidly impair judgment, damage the brain and trigger serious psychiatric conditions.
The discreet appearance of vape devices also makes enforcement and detection more challenging.
This issue demands a comprehensive national response involving government agencies, schools, parents, healthcare professionals, community organisations and the private sector.
Among the immediate measures that should be considered are:
*Intensifying nationwide enforcement against the manufacture, trafficking, online sale and distribution of synthetic drugs, including those concealed in vape liquids and cartridges.
*Strengthening border controls to prevent the smuggling of synthetic drug precursors.
*Expanding public education campaigns, especially in schools, colleges and universities, to educate young people on the dangers of synthetic drugs and drug-laced vape products.
*Increasing mental health screening for individuals undergoing drug rehabilitation so that those with dual diagnosis receive integrated treatment rather than separate interventions.
*Enhancing collaboration between Nada, the Health Ministry, educational institutions, law enforcement agencies and mental health professionals to provide coordinated care and rehabilitation.
*Equipping parents and teachers with the knowledge to recognise early warning signs of drug abuse and mental health problems before they escalate.
*Reviewing existing laws relating to vape products to ensure they cannot be exploited as a delivery system for dangerous synthetic substances.
Drug rehabilitation today must go beyond treating addiction alone. It must also address the psychological and psychiatric consequences of synthetic drug use.
Without integrated treatment, many recovering addicts will remain vulnerable to relapse, self-harm, violence, homelessness and involvement in crime.
The Alliance for a Safe Community (IKATAN) fully supports Nada's efforts in highlighting this growing threat.
We urge all stakeholders to recognise that preventing synthetic drug abuse is not solely a law enforcement responsibility, but a shared national responsibility involving families, schools, healthcare providers, employers, religious organisations and every member of society.
Malaysia cannot afford to lose another generation of young people to synthetic drugs and preventable mental illness.
We must act decisively, collectively and urgently before this crisis grows beyond control.
TAN SRI LEE LAM THYE
Chairman
Alliance For A Safe Community (IKATAN)
*The views expressed in this article are the author's own and do not necessarily reflect those of the New Straits Times
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johari: Political stability key to attracting investments, Maju Johor 2030
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Maju Johor 2030 agenda, which promises development and thousands of jobs, will not be achieved without a stable government with a clear mandate, says Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani.
Johari, who is the investment, trade and industry minister, said political stability was the key to attracting foreign investment into the state.
"If there is no political stability, we will have problems (attracting investors). Johor's contribution to the gross domestic product is very high compared with other states because its politics are stable," he said at the Wacana Kempas programme.
Johari said Johor's strong infrastructure was another advantage in attracting foreign investors, particularly those from Singapore.
"Investors look at infrastructure before considering what we have to offer. For data centre investments, they look at whether we have enough electricity and water," he said.
Citing data centre investments, Johari, who is also an Umno vice-president, said land constraints in Singapore had prompted companies there to expand into Johor.
"At first they were hesitant, but over time they saw that the Johor government is investor-friendly and stable. There is also ample land. So they came, hundreds came to invest.
"That is why we see RM110 billion in investments coming into Johor," he said.
He said Johoreans would decide next Saturday whether the state continued to enjoy political stability.
"Johor cannot develop without this (political stability)," he said at the event that was also attended by Barisan Nasional's Kempas candidate Datuk Ramlee Bohani and Dewan Negara Deputy Speaker Datuk Nur Jazlan Mohamed.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: King extends birthday greetings to Sultan of Kedah
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today extended birthday greetings to the Sultan of Kedah, Al-Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah, on the occasion of His Royal Highness' 84th birthday.
In a post on the official Facebook page of Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar, His Majesty prayed that Sultan Sallehuddin would be blessed with happiness, good health and a long life by Allah SWT.
"May Allah SWT bless His Royal Highness with happiness and grant him a long life," His Majesty said.
Meanwhile, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim also conveyed his heartfelt birthday wishes to Sultan Sallehuddin.
He prayed that Sultan Sallehuddin, the Sultanah of Kedah, Sultanah Maliha Tengku Ariff, and the entire Kedah royal family would continue to be blessed with Allah SWT's mercy and guidance, and be granted good health, well-being and enduring sovereignty.
"Daulat Tuanku. Dirgahayu Tuanku," he said in a Facebook post.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: Young voters remain Pas' biggest challenge, says Tuan Ibrahim
Article:
KOTA BARU: Young voters remain Pas' biggest challenge as it seeks to broaden its support ahead of the Johor state election, says deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man.
He said that although the party has made significant progress in expanding its influence beyond its traditional strongholds, winning the support of younger voters remains a key priority.
"About 40 per cent of the electorate are young voters. This is our challenge," said the Perikatan Nasional deputy chairman.
Tuan Ibrahim said Pas has also become more experienced in fielding candidates outside their home states, with selections based on the party's overall electoral strategy rather than geographical origin.
He said it was never easy to field an out-of-state candidate in a constituency.
"That is why the state leadership carefully assesses the needs of each state before deciding on candidates.
"We have done this before in Kelantan and Pahang by changing candidates, and the party now knows how to deploy candidates beyond their home states," he said.
He said Pas is also able to deploy experienced party workers and expertise to strengthen campaigns in other states.
"We strengthen the party's agenda by putting the right people in the right places. That includes sharing expertise and mobilising our machinery wherever it is needed," he said.
He said public sentiment, particularly among younger Malaysians, is increasingly favourable towards seeing Pas play a greater role at the federal level.
"God willing, the current mood among the people is that they want to see Pas govern at the federal level. This is not only among older voters but also among the younger generation."
On Pas' campaign strategy for the Johor election, Tuan Ibrahim said Perikatan Nasional will adopt a pragmatic approach by allowing its component parties to use their respective logos in the constituencies they contest.
He said Pas will use its own logo in the seats it contests, while Perikatan Nasional branding will be emphasised elsewhere to strengthen support for coalition candidates.
"It is not easy to ask Pas supporters to vote for the Barisan Nasional 'dacing' (scale). That is why we adopt a strategic approach in our campaign," he said.
Under PN, Pas is contesting 11 seats, Bersatu 16, the Malaysian Indian People's Party five, and Pejuang one. Parti Wawasan Negara and Gerakan are skipping the state polls.
Meanwhile, Johor Barisan Nasional and Pakatan will contest all 56 state seats.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nur Jazlan: No Pas-BN agreement in Johor, just a common opponent
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: There is no agreement between Pas and Barisan Nasional (BN) in Johor, and any support from the Islamic party for BN candidates is simply because both parties are contesting against Pakatan Harapan (PH), says a senior Umno leader.
"That is why, if there are Pas supporters who choose to support BN in certain seats, it is not because there is an agreement or a planned joint strategy," said Umno supreme council member Datuk Nur Jazlan Mohamed.
"It is simply because both are facing the same opponent," he said in an interview with Berita Harian.
Nur Jazlan said many people had forgotten that Pas and its now-fierce rival DAP were once coalition partners.
"There was Angkatan Perpaduan Ummah, and later Pas worked with DAP in various political coalitions for almost 20 years.
"So after Pas' relationship with DAP, and later PKR, broke down, they came to understand DAP's character," he said.
On traffic congestion, property prices and the rising cost of living, Nur Jazlan said Johor was facing challenges similar to those experienced by other developed states such as Selangor.
"Traffic congestion occurs there too despite the many highways that have been built.
"If we want a more advanced and vibrant economy, challenges such as more vehicles on the road and heavier traffic are inevitable," he said.
He said serious congestion in Johor was largely confined to the southern corridor, particularly Johor Baru and Kulai, and reflected the state's growing economy.
"If the economy were not growing and people could not afford vehicles, there would certainly be no congestion problem," he said.
Nur Jazlan said the BN government needed another five-year mandate to address these issues through continued infrastructure investment.
He also criticised what he described as PH's unfulfilled promise, made eight years ago, to develop southern Johor so residents would no longer need to cross into Singapore for work.
"To this day, even though the federal government is led by PH, I do not see that effort truly succeeding to the point where people working in Singapore have returned to work in Johor," he said.
On claims that high voter turnout favours PH while lower turnout benefits BN, Nur Jazlan said that the narrative was primarily aimed at helping DAP rather than PKR or Amanah following the party's setback in Sabah.
"DAP is worried that what happened in Sabah will happen in Johor. That is why they are placing so much emphasis on voter turnout, particularly in areas with many Chinese voters," he said.
He said the Sabah factor — where a campaign to reject Peninsula-based parties influenced the election outcome — did not apply in Johor, although he believed DAP remained anxious after the experience.
On winning over non-Malay voters, Nur Jazlan said BN's track record spoke for itself.
"For the people of Johor, the most important thing is the economy.
"If they believe BN is capable of strengthening the state's economy, creating more job opportunities and improving their quality of life, they will support BN," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Nur Hafiz pledges yearly report card if elected Machap rep
Article:
KLUANG: Pakatan Harapan candidate for Machap, Nur Hafiz Roslan, has pledged to publish a yearly performance report card if elected.
The Amanah Youth executive member said the move is part of his "people-first" administration, which focuses on healthcare access, rural infrastructure gaps and long-standing Felda housing issues.
Nur Hafiz said his campaign is rooted in direct engagement with residents on the ground, particularly elderly voters and Felda communities who, he claimed, continue to face unresolved structural challenges despite past interventions.
Among his key proposals is a community-based healthcare outreach initiative, which he said would collaborate with the Health Ministry and local clinic networks to support elderly residents whose children work outside the constituency.
"We will set up a medical outreach team so that weekly visits can be carried out.
"Many of the elderly here are living alone while their children are working elsewhere. Their worry is real, especially when communication coverage is weak," he said when met after attending the "PMX Santai Sarapan" programme in Simpang Renggam, near here, today.
The Amanah Youth executive committee member also said Felda-related issues remain a central concern in Machap, particularly involving second-generation settlers and housing allocation matters, which he claimed have remained unresolved for years.
He said infrastructure development in certain areas does not reflect ground realities, adding that basic connectivity issues such as mobile coverage remain a daily challenge in rural zones.
"On paper, infrastructure may look complete, but on the ground, people are still struggling with coverage and access.
"That is something we need to fix with the relevant agencies."
He also highlighted concerns about road safety in Felda areas, claiming that accidents linked to poor lighting along access roads had resulted in fatalities in recent years, and urged immediate attention from the authorities.
Nur Hafiz said that if given the mandate, he would introduce a yearly performance report card to allow voters to assess the delivery of campaign promises.
"The people are king. If I am elected, I will present what has been achieved every year so that people can judge my performance."
He added that the final days of campaigning should focus on policy and solutions
rather than political confrontation, calling on supporters to maintain a respectful tone.
"We should focus on what we can offer to the people of Machap. Campaigning must be about solutions, not conflict."
Nur Hafiz is facing a straight fight with caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, who is also Johor BN chairman.
Onn Hafiz is seeking a second term in Machap after winning the seat in the 2022 Johor election with a majority of 6,543 votes, securing 11,029 votes in a four-cornered contest.
Polling is scheduled for July 11, with early voting set for July 7.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: DAP never disputes Malay rights and supports a government for all races
Article:
MUAR, July 5 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim stressed that DAP has never disputed the rights of the Malays, thus refuting the narrative of certain quarters who are trying to portray the party as a racist organisation.
Anwar, who is also the chairman of Pakatan Harapan (PH), said as proof, DAP has never objected to its intention to gazette 20.23 hectares of land in Bandar Malaysia as Malay Reserve Land.
However, he said that as the leader of the unity government, it is his responsibility to be fair to all races to ensure that the country’s prosperity can be enjoyed together.
“We do programmes (for the Malay community), Bumiputeras, increase allocations for Perbadanan Usahawan Nasional Berhad (PUNB), it (DAP) has no problem.
“But I have to ensure that programmes with the Chinese are not blocked. Poor Chinese should be helped, poor Indians should be helped, the majority of poor Malays should be helped,” he said at the Meeting Anwar Programme at a restaurant hall here today.
Commenting further, Anwar said that despite the country’s current economic boom, the profits obtained should be distributed fairly to all levels of society and not for the personal interests of certain individuals.
He said the country’s economic growth should be translated into the well-being of the people through the provision of comfortable homes, good basic facilities and improved quality of education.
“The economy is growing, we want it to be distributed fairly, ensure that the people’s homes, stalls, basic facilities, quality of education are improved.
“I want children to understand, power is a trust to be used, not to enrich yourself. Politics is not about enriching oneself. Politics is about service,” he said.
The Johor state election is seeing 172 candidates contesting in 56 state assembly seats with polling day set for July 11 and early voting on July 7.
For the latest news on the 16th Johor state election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor — Bernama

Title: Thunderstorms and heavy rain to hit KL and several states until 4pm, says MetMalaysia
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 5 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds expected to hit Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya and eight states until 4pm today.
MetMalaysia, in a statement, said the warning covers Kelantan (Jeli, Kuala Krai and Gua Musang), Terengganu (Besut, Hulu Terengganu, Dungun and Kemaman), and Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Raub, Bentong, Pekan and Rompin).
The warning also applies to Selangor, involving the districts of Hulu Selangor, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat, Hulu Langat and Sepang.
Adverse weather conditions are also forecast in Negeri Sembilan (Jelebu, Seremban and Port Dickson) and Johor (Batu Pahat, Kluang, Mersing, Pontian, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru).
In Sabah, the affected areas are the interior (Sipitang, Kuala Penyu, Beaufort and Tambunan), the west coast (Papar, Penampang and Ranau), and Kudat (Kota Marudu).
Meanwhile, in Sarawak, the warning covers Kuching, Serian, Samarahan, Sri Aman, Betong (Pusa and Betong), Kapit (Song, Kapit and Bukit Mabong), and Limbang. — Bernama

Title: Sultan Sallehuddin marks 84th birthday by conferring top honours on military and police chiefs
Article:
ALOR SETAR, 5 July — Chief of Defence Forces (CDF) General Tan Sri Malek Razak Sulaiman and Inspector-General of Police (IGP) Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail, head the list of recipients of honours and medals in conjunction with the 84th Birthday Celebration of the Sultan of Kedah, Al Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah, at Istana Anak Bukit here today.
Sultan Sallehuddin consented to the conferment of the Darjah Dato’ Seri Setia Pahlawan Yang Amat Dihormati (S.S.P.K), which carries the title Dato’ Seri Pahlawan on both prominent figures.
For the conferment of the Orang Besar Enam Belas, Datuk Syed Khairol Anuar Syed Abidin was bestowed the title Dato’ Jaya Perkasa, while Datuk Tan Hong Eng received the title Dato’ Jaya Dermawan.
As for the Orang Besar Tiga Puluh Dua, Datuk Mohamad Che Nai was conferred the title Dato’ Johan Bakti, while Datuk Seri Boon Som Inong received the title Dato’ Biram Jaya. Meanwhile, Kedah legislative assembly speaker Zubir Ahmad was conferred the Darjah Dato’ Setia Sultan Sallehuddin Kedah (D.S.S.S), which carries the title Dato’.
Four individuals received the Darjah Dato’ Setia Diraja Kedah (D.S.D.K), which carries the title Dato’. They are Syarikat Air Darul Aman (Sada) chief executive officer (CEO) Radzuan Osman, Alor Setar Mayor Datuk Abdul Gafar Yahya, Malaysian Immigration Department director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban, and Fisheries Development Authority of Malaysia (LKIM) director-general Dr Abdul Salim Tajudin.
Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah was conferred the Darjah Dato’ Setia Pahlawan (D.D.S.P), which also carries the title Dato’.
The Darjah Setia Sultan Sallehuddin Kedah (S.S.S) was awarded to two recipients, namely Kedah state executive council member Major (Rtd) Mansor Zakaria and PAS Youth Chief Afnan Hamimi Taib Azamudden.
A total of nine individuals received the Darjah Setia Diraja Kedah (S.D.K), including Kedah State Islamic Religious Affairs Department director Syeikh Mohamad Subhi Abdullah, Langkawi Development Authority (Lada) deputy CEO (Management) Maznim Ismail, Kedah Public Works Department director Ir Mohd Fisal Ismail, and Muda Agricultural Development Authority (Mada) general manager Zulkifli Romli.
Also receiving the S.D.K were Health Ministry Dental Health Programme deputy director Dr Jamaliah Omar, Sultanah Bahiyah Hospital head of Neurosurgery Department Dr Adam Mohd Zakaria, the hospital’s senior consultant general surgeon Dr Manisekar K. Subramaniam, Real Estate and Housing Developers Association (Rehda) Kedah/Perlis chairman Yeoh Su Guan, and Bismi Empire Sdn Bhd president Jaffri Ahmad.
In addition, Sultan Sallehuddin consented to the conferment of the Ahli Mahkota Kedah (A.M.K) to 31 recipients, Ahli Setia Pahlawan (A.S.P) to five recipients, Bintang Perkhidmatan Cemerlang Kedah (B.C.K) to 31 recipients, and Bintang Kebaktian Masyarakat (B.K.M) to 43 recipients.
His Royal Highness also awarded the Ahli Cemerlang Semangat Jerai Kedah (A.S.K) to five recipients, Pingat Perkhidmatan Cemerlang Kedah (P.C.K) to 55 recipients, Pingat Jasa Kebaktian (P.J.K) to 40 recipients, and Pingat Perkhidmatan Lama (P.P.L) to 15 recipients. — Bernama
